growth_records.cite: Wang C et al. Epsilon-poly-L-lysine: Recent Advances in Biomanufacturing and Applications. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2021). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2021.748976. [PMC8506220]
growth_records.snippet: Streptomyces albulus 11011A, fed batch fermentation 120 h, glucose + yeast extract, AEC-resistant mutant -> 5.7 g/L (Hiraki et al. (1998))
growth_records.notes: Value CITED in this review (Wang et al. 2021, Front Bioeng Biotechnol) FROM primary paper Hiraki et al. (1998); not measured in the review. Volumetric productivity derived = 5.7 g/L / 120 h = 0.0475 g/L/h. Medium given only qualitatively (glucose + yeast extract), not quantified -> medium_composition_stated:false. No growth rate, biomass, or substrate uptake reported here; epsilon-PL is the excreted product.
